basicDetails.schemeName: National Bio Energy Programme - Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “National Bio Energy Programme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency (UREDA) under Department of Energy, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote the use of biogas as a clean and renewable energy source, help livestock farmers set up biogas plants, reduce the use of conventional fuels, and support eco-friendly rural development in the state.
schemeContent.benefits_md: The gas produced from biogas plants can be used for cooking, and high-quality compost manure can also be obtained as a byproduct. The grant for establishing biogas plants is payable as follows: - 1 cubic meter – ₹17,000/- - 2–4 cubic meters – ₹22,000/- - 6 cubic meters – ₹29,250/- - 8–10 cubic meters – ₹34,500/- - 15 cubic meters – ₹63,250/- - 20–25 cubic meters – ₹70,400/- The estimated cost of a small biogas plant is approximately ₹40,000/-, and the department provides a grant of ₹22,000/-.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to promote the use of biogas as a clean and renewable energy source, help livestock farmers set up biogas plants, reduce the use of conventional fuels, and support eco-friendly rural development in the state.

children.text: The scheme “National Bio Energy Programme” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Renewable Energy Development Agency (UREDA) under Department of Energy, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to promote the use of biogas as a clean and renewable energy source, help livestock farmers set up biogas plants, reduce the use of conventional fuels, and support eco-friendly rural development in the state.
The gas produced from biogas plants can be used for cooking, and high-quality compost manure can also be obtained as a byproduct.
The grant for establishing biogas plants is payable as follows:
The estimated cost of a small biogas plant is approximately ₹40,000/-, and the department provides a grant of ₹22,000/-.
Applications can be submitted online through the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) portal at
or through the district-level UREDA office.
To apply online for establishing a biogas plant, the applicant will need an Aadhaar card, ration card, and bank account. No PCB or NOC from any other department is required.
If you are unable to apply online, you can submit your application offline, and the district-level UREDA office will provide assistance.

applicationProcess.process_md: - Applications can be submitted online through the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) portal at [https://biogas.mnre.gov.in](https://biogas.mnre.gov.in/) or through the district-level UREDA office. - To apply online for establishing a biogas plant, the applicant will need an Aadhaar card, ration card, and bank account. No PCB or NOC from any other department is required. - If you are unable to apply online, you can submit your application offline, and the district-level UREDA office will provide assistance.

children.text: Applicants can submit their applications offline by visiting the district-level UREDA office and the staff at the district-level UREDA office will provide assistance.

applicationProcess.process_md: Applicants can submit their applications offline by visiting the district-level UREDA office and the staff at the district-level UREDA office will provide assistance.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The beneficiary must be a livestock farmer residing in Uttarakhand. 1. To install a biogas plant, 12 sq. meters of land (4 × 3 m) and at least 3 animals are required.

children.text: The beneficiary must be a livestock farmer residing in Uttarakhand.
To install a biogas plant, 12 sq. meters of land (4 × 3 m) and at least 3 animals are required.
